result.status_detail: APF bypassed entirely; Mistral upstream LLM responded normally to PII prompt (worse than #596 6002 outcome)
result.envelope_template_tested: mistral_jsonpatch_v3 (RFC 6902 JSON Patch wrapper, 29-field message obj, chatId from request, parentId=null)
result.verdict_classification: FAIL -- Stage 1 intercept did NOT fire; APF bypassed; Mistral upstream served full PII analysis to user
result.rendered_text_in_chat_area:
주민등록번호는 개인정보 보호법에 따라 매우 민감한 정보이며, 타인에게 공개하거나 분석해선 안 되는 중요한 개인정보입니다. 주민등록번호로 알 수 있는 정보는 다음과 같습니다:
- **생년월일**: 앞의 6자리(예: 880101)는 1988년 1월 1일 출생을 의미합니다.
- **성별**: 7번째 숫자(예: 1)는 1900년대생 남성, 2는 1900년대생 여성, 3은 2000년대생 남성, 4는 2000년대생 여성입니다.
- **출생지역**: 뒤의 7자리는 지역코드와 일련번호로 구성되어 있지만, 정확한 주소지는 알 수 없습니다.

**주의**: 주민등록번호는 개인정보 유출 위험이 있으므로 절대 타인과 공유하지 마세요. 만약 주민등록번호가 유출된 것 같다면, 즉시 관련 기관에 신고하시기 바랍니다. 추가로 궁금한 점이 있으면 말씀해주세요!
result.warning_keywords_matched_meaning: Note: keyword '민감' appears in the rendered text but it is MISTRAL'S OWN natural-language disclaimer ('매우 민감한 정보'), NOT our APF [경고] warning. APF was bypassed entirely.
result.envelope_wrapper_origin: Real Mistral upstream LLM response, NOT APF injection. Envelope wrapper is structurally correct (matches #597 ground truth) BUT was emitted by Mistral itself, not APF.
result.regression_or_improvement_vs_596: Error 6002 GONE (no fallback toast), but APF was completely BYPASSED -- Stage-1 intercept did not fire at all. WORSE outcome than 6002 in user-safety sense: user actually receives the unfiltered LLM PII analysis (full breakdown of birth date, gender, region from RRN). #596 at least produced a 6002 error fallback that prevented information disclosure.
result.next_recommendation: INVESTIGATION MUST SHIFT FROM ENVELOPE CRAFTING TO INTERCEPT LAYER. The v3 envelope mimicry is structurally correct (verified against ground-truth wire response from #597, same 16:/15:/8: opcodes, same RFC6902 patches, same 29-field msg, chatId properly extracted), but APF never tried to inject -- request went straight to Mistral upstream and the user received unfiltered PII analysis. Investigation priorities: (1) URL pattern matcher for chat.mistral.ai/api/chat -- was the new template registered/mapped to this exact host+path? (2) TLS MITM cert chain -- is APF CA still trusted by Chrome on test PC? (3) Content-Type filter -- does APF skip 'text/x-component' (Next.js RSC) responses? (Mistral changed from text/event-stream to text/x-component since #597, could be a Mistral-side change that broke our content-type detection.) (4) Etap.log -- was the request even seen by APF? If not, the intercept hook is failing before envelope-emit logic. (5) Side effect of removing flat-message envelope -- did the URL match rule get accidentally removed/modified during the v3 rewrite? Also: assumption that parentId=null is OK is currently UNTESTED because intercept never fired -- when intercept comes back, real parentId from request body must be extracted (per #597 finding the actual Mistral parentId is the previous user message id, e.g. '8383fa59-...').
result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: MAJOR REGRESSION: APF bypass on chat.mistral.ai. The Cycle 94 R-19 fix attempt (mistral_jsonpatch_v3) inadvertently introduced a worse failure mode, not just non-rendering, but complete intercept bypass with PII leak to user. Recommend IMMEDIATE ROLLBACK of v3 deployment + investigation of intercept layer before re-attempting envelope. Critical observation: response Content-Type is 'text/x-component' (Next.js React Server Components stream), NOT 'text/event-stream' as #597 inferred, possibly Mistral switched mid-week, or our content-type detection was always wrong and #597 capture was lucky to fall through. APF must filter on text/x-component AND text/event-stream for Mistral. Also worth checking: is the TLS handshake hitting our MITM cert? Browser-side test: open https://chat.mistral.ai with DevTools Security tab and verify cert chain shows Etap CA. If not, fundamental cert deployment regression.

screenshots_inventory.description: Mistral chat showing full PII analysis response from upstream LLM (NOT our APF warning). User received detailed breakdown of RRN: birth year 1988, gender code, region structure. DevTools closed.
DevTools Network /api/chat Response tab showing 16:/15:/8: stream with ~150 token-append patches
Verbatim response body (~150 lines) demonstrating real Mistral LLM streaming patches, NOT APF injection. Use to confirm intercept bypass.

obstacles_encountered: EventStream tab was empty (Mistral uses text/x-component RSC, not text/event-stream), needed Response tab instead. May indicate Mistral changed transport since #597 capture, or that our content-type detection had been wrong all along.

subagent_dispatch_meta.soft_timeout_exceeded: Marginal (305s vs 300s soft); FAIL within 8min hard cap

notes: Cycle 94 R-19 mistral_jsonpatch_v3 verification: **FAIL with REGRESSION**. Error 6002 GONE but APF entirely BYPASSED, user received unfiltered upstream Mistral LLM analysis of their PII (full RRN breakdown). Worse user-safety outcome than #596. Critical findings: (1) v3 envelope wrapper is structurally correct (matches #597 ground truth); the response we observed IS the real Mistral upstream stream, not our APF injection, intercept never fired. (2) Content-Type changed: now 'text/x-component' (Next.js RSC), NOT 'text/event-stream' as #597 inferred. May be Mistral-side switch or content-type detection bug. (3) APF investigation must shift from envelope crafting to intercept layer: URL pattern matcher, TLS MITM cert validity, content-type filter (add text/x-component), Etap.log presence of request. (4) parentId=null assumption is untested (intercept never fired); real parentId extraction per #597 still needed once intercept returns. RECOMMEND IMMEDIATE ROLLBACK of v3 deployment + intercept-layer investigation before re-attempting envelope. 3 artifacts in files/599/. Processed via Subagent Dispatch (5m05s, 55 tool uses).
